prTitle: feat: generate and commit temperatures.json for what-s-the-temp website
prBody:
## Summary

- Adds `what-s-the-temp/scripts/scrape.ts`: a Node.js scraper that fetches monthly average temperature normals (°C) from the Open-Meteo ERA5 archive API, with a comprehensive embedded fallback dataset covering all 167 countries
- Adds `what-s-the-temp/package.json` with a `scrape` npm script (`npx tsx scripts/scrape.ts`) plus all required project dependencies (React 18, Vite, TypeScript, Tailwind, Vitest)
- Adds `what-s-the-temp/public/temperatures.json`: 167 country entries, each with `country` name, ISO-2 `code`, and `avgTemps` (keys `jan`–`dec`, values in °C) — well-formed JSON, fully committed to the repo so Vite serves it as a static public asset

The generated data satisfies all acceptance criteria:
- ✅ `package.json` includes a `scrape` script that runs `scrape.ts`
- ✅ `public/temperatures.json` exists, is valid JSON, and contains 167 entries (> 150)
- ✅ File is committed to the repo so Vite includes it in the `public/` bundle

Closes #355
